code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    
    # Forward rate bias: trade the carry difference between EUR and GBP
    # Use weekly data to capture persistent interest rate differentials
    
    lookback = params['lookback'][0]
    entry_z = params['entry_z'][0]
    exit_z = params['exit_z'][0]
    
    # Calculate rolling mean and std of weekly returns
    df['returns'] = df['Close'].pct_change()
    df['mean_ret'] = df['returns'].rolling(lookback).mean()
    df['std_ret'] = df['returns'].rolling(lookback).std()
    
    # Z-score of weekly returns (proxy for deviation from carry equilibrium)
    df['z_score'] = (df['returns'] - df['mean_ret']) / df['std_ret']
    
    # Entry: when z-score is extremely negative (GBP weak, EUR strong) -> short EUR/GBP
    # Exit: when z-score reverts to near zero
    df['signal'] = 0
    
    # Short condition (sell EUR/GBP): extreme negative z-score means EUR is overbought vs GBP
    short_entry = df['z_score'] < -entry_z
    df.loc[short_entry, 'signal'] = -1
    
    # Exit short when z-score rises back to exit threshold
    exit_short = (df['z_score'] > -exit_z) & (df['z_score'] < exit_z)
    df.loc[exit_short & (df['signal'].shift(1) == -1), 'signal'] = 0
    
    # Long condition (buy EUR/GBP): extreme positive z-score means GBP is overbought vs EUR
    long_entry = df['z_score'] > entry_z
    df.loc[long_entry, 'signal'] = 1
    
    # Exit long when z-score falls back to exit threshold
    exit_long = (df['z_score'] > -exit_z) & (df['z_score'] < exit_z)
    df.loc[exit_long & (df['signal'].shift(1) == 1), 'signal'] = 0
    
    return df['signal']
rationale: The forward rate bias anomaly suggests that currencies with higher interest rates tend to depreciate over time, but deviations from this equilibrium due to momentum chasing create predictable mean-reverting opportunities in EUR/GBP on weekly timescales.

self_critique.why_this_might_be_noise: Interest rate differentials change slowly and the carry trade is well-known, so any exploitable signal may have been arbitraged away in recent decades.
self_critique.what_would_disprove_this: If the strategy fails during periods of diverging monetary policy (e.g., one central bank hiking while the other cuts), or when risk-off sentiment dominates and carry trades unwind violently.
